content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA consisting of NNN **distinct** positive integers.

Find the **smallest** positive integer ddd such that there exists **no** pair of integers (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) (1≤i<j≤N)(1\le i\lt j \le N)(1≤i<j≤N) where abs(Ai−Aj)abs(A_i - A_j)abs(Ai​−Aj​) is divisible by ddd.

In other words, d∤abs(Ai−Aj)d \nmid abs(A_i - A_j)d∤abs(Ai​−Aj​) for all (1≤i<j≤N)(1\le i \lt j\le N)(1≤i<j≤N).


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integers NNN — the number of the array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated distinct integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots , A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ denoting the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output the smallest positive integer ddd such that d∤(Ai−Aj)d \nmid (A_i - A_j)d∤(Ai​−Aj​) for all (1≤i<j≤N)(1\le i \lt j\le N)(1≤i<j≤N).
constraints: The sums of NNN and max(A)max(A)max(A) over all test cases does not exceed 3⋅1053 \cdot 10 ^ 53⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The possible differences are abs(A1−A2)=1,abs(A1−A3)=2,abs(A2−A3)=1abs(A_1 - A_2) = 1, abs(A_1 - A_3) = 2, abs(A_2 - A_3) = 1abs(A1​−A2​)=1,abs(A1​−A3​)=2,abs(A2​−A3​)=1. The smallest positive integer not dividing any of these differences is 333.
Test case 222: The possible differences are abs(A1−A2)=1,abs(A1−A3)=9,abs(A2−A3)=8abs(A_1 - A_2) = 1, abs(A_1 - A_3) = 9, abs(A_2 - A_3) = 8abs(A1​−A2​)=1,abs(A1​−A3​)=9,abs(A2​−A3​)=8. The smallest positive integer not dividing any of these differences is 555.
Test case 333: The possible differences are abs(A1−A2)=2,abs(A1−A3)=9,abs(A2−A3)=7abs(A_1 - A_2) = 2, abs(A_1 - A_3) = 9, abs(A_2 - A_3) = 7abs(A1​−A2​)=2,abs(A1​−A3​)=9,abs(A2​−A3​)=7. The smallest positive integer not dividing any of these differences is 444.
